vulnerability.title: Enable Kernel Parameter to Use Reverse Path Filtering on all IPv4 Interfaces
vulnerability.description: To set the runtime status of the net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter kernel parameter, run the following command: $ sudo sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter=1 To make sure that the setting is persistent, add the following line to a file in the directory /etc/sysctl.d: net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter = 1
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then # Comment out any occurrences of net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter from /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf files for f in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do # skip systemd-sysctl symlink (/etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf -> /etc/sysctl.conf) if [[ "$(readlink -f "$f")" == "/etc/sysctl.conf" ]]; then continue; fi matching_list=$(grep -P '^(?!#).*[\s]*net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter.*$' $f | uniq ) if ! test -z "$matching_list"; then while IFS= read -r entry; do escaped_entry=$(sed -e 's|/|\\/|g' <<< "$entry") # comment out "net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter" matches to preserve user data sed -i --follow-symlinks "s/^${escaped_entry}$/# &/g" $f done <<< "$matching_list" fi done # # Set sysctl co

triage_decision.reason: Enabling the reverse path filter (rp_filter) helps prevent IP address spoofing by ensuring that incoming packets are received on the interface that the system would use to send a reply, thus mitigating certain network-based attacks.

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for sysctl_net_ipv4_conf_all_rp_filter (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_sysctl_net_ipv4_conf_all_rp_filter). Could not verify.

We are to enable kernel parameter net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter = 1.
 Steps:
 1. Check if the parameter is already set in /etc/sysctl.conf or in files under /etc/sysctl.d/.
 2. If it exists, set it to 1 (uncomment if necessary).
 3. If it does not exist, add it to a file in /etc/sysctl.d/ (we can use /etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf or create a new file, but note the remediation script says to avoid editing /etc/sysctl.conf directly and use /etc/sysctl.d).
 4. Also set the runtime value with sysctl -w.

 However, note the rules:
 - We must use read_file before modifying.
 - We must use run_cmd for exactly one command at a time (no chaining).
 - We must not duplicate lines.

 First, we'll check the current runtime value and then look for the setting in config files.
